key_decisions_and_rationale: Native Grok is an additional provider, not a replacement for MuAPI Grok, to preserve existing user workflows.
The user-facing native label is Grok Imagine 1.5 (server-native) so Karim can distinguish it from the MuAPI option.
Grok supports selectable 480p and 720p in this implementation because the local wrapper supports those resolutions.
Use the branch gateway on 19335 for 19400 testing because an older gateway occupied 19334 without the new Grok capability.
For the future asset-library plan, .native-media is the durable native media source of truth; browser localStorage remains fallback for legacy MuAPI entries.
Future delete is jobId-only. The server derives assetId from jobs.json and performs realpath containment checks before deleting .native-media/assets/<asset-id>.
Future delete should tombstone jobs in jobs.json with atomic temp-file plus rename writes, not remove provenance.
Future V1 does not delete .native-media/uploads because uploads may be source/reference inputs and need a separate unreferenced-upload cleanup policy.
Future library GET skips deleted/tombstoned/fake jobs by default and merges server/local UI history by url, then jobId, then id, with server fields winning.
Do not apply any Gemini audit recommendations for the asset-library plan because the Gemini run failed before producing a report.
issues_discovered: Original upload attempt failed at 100 percent with MuAPI 403 because native Grok upload was using the wrong upload path. This was fixed in the branch commits.
Cancel smoke initially left a nested Grok process behind; fixed by forwarding signals from grok_imagine_video.py to the nested Grok process group.
The wrong GLM Multica agent path failed before audit because it used the non-working provider path; issue MER-183 was closed.
Gemini 3.5 Flash Multica issue MER-184 failed before audit with no useful report; it was closed.
Asset visibility across branches is not solved by the current implementation because Studio history is still browser localStorage-based and capped; the durable follow-up plan addresses it.
If the gateway starts from a different cwd without NATIVE_MEDIA_ROOT, it will use a different .native-media folder and the UI may look empty even though assets exist elsewhere.
open_questions: Does Karim's current multi-reference Grok Imagine UI test pass on port 19400?
Before merging to main, are there generated assets in other worktrees or previous feature branches that need to be merged into this repo's .native-media store?
Should the asset-library/delete/copy plan be implemented immediately after Karim finishes testing, or kept as the next separate feature branch?
next_steps: Keep the branch app available on http://127.0.0.1:19400 for Karim's multi-reference test.
When Karim reports multi-reference results, update docs/plans/grok-imagine-video-provider-task-list.md and context with pass/fail details.
If multi-reference fails, diagnose with Graphify first, then narrow rg/sed reads, and use native executor subagents for any code changes.
Before merge to main, back up .native-media/jobs.json, .native-media/assets, and .native-media/uploads.
Before merge to main, decide and document a stable NATIVE_MEDIA_ROOT so generated media does not disappear from the interface after branch changes.

If asked to implement the asset-library/delete/copy feature, start from docs/plans/native-media-asset-library-delete-copy-plan.md and use tests for delete containment, tombstones, merge behavior, server-down fallback, and UI copy/delete states.
After any meaningful code changes, run Graphify refresh with graphify update . --force.
Do not merge until Karim finishes testing on 19400 and approves.
